info.name: Tourism Booking System API
info.description:
Postman collection for the Laravel Tourism Booking System API.

How to use:
1. Select the "Tourism Booking System Production" environment, or rely on the collection variables.
2. Run "Admin Login - Create Token" for admin-only endpoints such as package, promo package, tourist spot, booking approval, and payment management.
3. Run "Tourist Login - Create Token" for tourist booking/payment flows.
4. Login requests automatically save Bearer `token`, `admin_access_token`, `tourist_access_token`, and current IDs.
5. For file upload requests, choose a local file in the `image_file` or `proof_file` form-data row before sending.

Note: Requests use the full Railway production URL directly, so they work even if no Postman environment is selected.

request.description: Creates a tourist Sanctum personal access token. This request saves the token automatically as `token` and `tourist_access_token`. Run this before tourist booking/payment requests.
Creates an admin Sanctum personal access token. This request saves the token automatically as `token` and `admin_access_token`. Run this before admin-only requests.

item.name: Create Extra Token

exec: pm.test('Extra token is created and saved', function () {

item.description: Creates a second token using the currently saved Bearer token. This saves the new token id as `token_id`, which is the safe target for Revoke Token by ID.

item.name: List Tokens

exec: const extra = body.tokens.find(token => token.name === 'Extra Postman Token');

item.description: Lists personal access tokens for the current account. If an `Extra Postman Token` exists, its id is saved as `token_id` for revoke testing.

item.name: Revoke Token by ID

exec: pm.test('Token is revoked by id', function () {

item.description: Revokes the token id stored in `token_id`. Recommended flow: Login -> Create Extra Token -> Revoke Token by ID. This avoids revoking your current login token by mistake.

item.name: Revoke All Tokens

request.description: Danger: revokes every token for the currently authenticated account. Run login again after this.

item.description: Revokes every token for the logged-in account. After this, run Login again before calling protected endpoints.

item.name: Logout - Revoke Current Token
item.description: Revokes only the current Bearer token. After this, run Login again before protected requests.

exec: pm.test('Public package is returned', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });
